meaning: n.检疫,隔离,(政治或商业上的)封锁,检疫期间vt.检疫,使在政治或商业上孤立
examples: It is said that all ships coming from abroad should be performed quarantine.
She was sent home and put in quarantine.
Our tools for surveillance and quarantine in the U.S. have also gotten a lot better.
There were only 27 quarantine breaches reported between 1964 and 2003, according to government records.
